frameworks.name: tiny recurrent neural networks
frameworks.one_line: 1-4 unit RNNs discover cognitive algorithms underlying reward-learning decisions

frameworks.name: dynamical systems analysis
frameworks.one_line: trained RNN dynamics interpreted as cognitive mechanisms via dynamical systems concepts

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: normative framework whose handcrafted simplicity limits realistic behaviour capture

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: classical normative reward-learning models outperformed by tiny RNNs on choice prediction

core_question: Can small recurrent neural networks discover and reveal the cognitive algorithms governing biological reward-learning decisions?
key_assumptions: individual choices reflect a low-dimensional cognitive algorithm
small RNNs can express the relevant computations without overfitting
trained network dynamics are interpretable as cognitive mechanisms
manipulations: network size (1 to 4 hidden units vs larger networks)
model class (tiny RNN vs classical cognitive models)
task domain across six reward-learning tasks in animals and humans
key_findings: 1-4 unit RNNs often outperform classical cognitive models in predicting individual choices
tiny RNNs match the predictive accuracy of much larger networks
dynamical systems analysis of trained RNNs reveals choice-behaviour mechanisms
approach estimates the dimensionality of behaviour and characterises meta-RL agents
interpretations: tiny RNNs discover cognitive algorithms while remaining interpretable, bypassing handcrafted model cycles
a unified comparison of cognitive models becomes possible via shared dynamical-systems language
